In my case, if I wanted scientific accuracy, I wouldn't be watching a Hollywood movie about creatures we can only speculate about.

==Spoilers==

The movie had the CEO of Biosyn looking like Tim Cook from Apple with bill gates's agenda (the genetically modified grains stuff), 
Henry Wu allegorizing a Wuhan Lab scientist with his super locust(covid IRL) but with a redemption ark cause gotta get it past Chinese censor board?, 
Oh they even had Elon Musk's "hyperloop" lmao.

The clone kid is not actually a clone but a GMO baby birthed by the original daughter who didn't actually die in an accident but had a rare disease she fixed by having a baby version of herself without the disease.

What else? Oh Giganotosaurus gets taken down by Rexy and a T-rex sized Erlikosaurus. The Actual Antagonist of the movies are GMO Locust. 

Not kidding yall. 

Old Cast:
Grant and Ellie visit biosyn at a biosyn Isle to collect evidence that ties the GMO locust to biosyn- Malcom already works for biosyn and helps them get the evidence- in between the clone kid is abducted by biosyn and brought to the island cause WU wants to study her to reverse his mistake with the locust. They both end up crossing each other's paths trying to escape biosyn. Claire and Grady hook up with a smuggler to get to the isle and rescue the the clone kid aka their adopted daughter. 

Dinosaurs are used more of a plot device in the movie- There are raptors who like indo killed anyone who a laser is pointed at, The feather raptor encounter happens between the smuggler and Grady, Claire has an encounter Erlikosaurus, and when they're all together the Giganotosaurus is after them.

My entire childhood (i would say) was/is based around my love for dinosaurs and that was all made possible by this movie franchise. i am in absolute awe of what will happen and this is just blowing my mind. I dont care if the plot sucks, i just want to watch the movie. I love the series and i thank universal for giving me an amazing childhood and the franchise will ALWAYS have a place in my heart. Thank you so much for doing this, to the cast, crew and everyone involved i cannot thank you enough. I am so happy and this trailer sends chills down my spine and is the ONLY trailer to ever make me cry of joy. whilst i am unbelaiveably excited to watch this movie, i am devastated how this may be the final movie. I will cherish this. I am so grateful to have the og cast back and i am still shaking after watching this. Again, thank you to the moon and back and have an absolutely wonderful life. Thank you.

The change to portraying the dinosaurs as monsters rather than living, intelligent animals is the biggest transgression of the sequels as it fundamentally changes the audience's point of view, something which made the original Jurassic Park so successful. In JP1 you're not privy to anything the characters aren't; you're going through the experience WITH them. That's a deliberate, important creative decision by Spielberg. In the sequels. you're on the side of the "puppeteers", to use a timely comparison. You're now (as a viewer) more omniscient, instead of sympathetic. Criminal.

The Quetzalcoatlus that attacks the plane while it's flying above a mountain range shouldn't have been able to get anywhere near it due to its own sheer mass and bone density.

Despite being being portrayed as semi-aquatic with webbed feet, Pyroraptor portrayed lacks several features that semi-aquatic birds have such as the streamlined and waterproof feathers seen in cormorants and penguins. Likewise, with its large exposed face, hands, feet, and stomach it would lose heat quickly in a freezing environment, especially when in freezing waters. The real animal potentially did have some features to make it a competent swimmer, as it lived in what was the the time an archipelago of islands, but these wouldn't directly translate to cold water dwelling.

Saw this in an ad earlier and I had to stop and watch. So Blue's baby got kidnapped and Blue's most definitely a mom the fact she's mad at Owen for it even though it's not his fault. That's great. 

We gotta get her back while trying to keep out of the way of saurians that a bunch of other people had the bright idea to make. That's fine. 

One of said saurians is a feathered raptor that can swim like a sea serpent, ironically fulfilling something that I had been imagining for a while. That's unexpected, but I think I found my new second favorite dino after the Indoraptor. 

Wu is finally acknowledging that creating dinosaurs and allowing the whole world to get their hands on them so they can run amok among us was a terrible mistake. That's good. Hope he doesn't die.

And we have the real biggest and baddest carnivore to ever live, the Giganotosaurus - a dinosaur that I only ever viewed with like a mythical status, since I've never really seen it in action before - not a hybrid this time, wreaking havoc and likely posing a JP3 Spinosaurus-level threat (hopefully we see that one though I doubt it). That's fantastic.

All sarcasm by the way in case you couldn't guess, minus the serpent and Wu parts. 😂Absolutely cannot wait for this movie: if I wasn't able to get my family to see the Sonic Movie 2, I am definitely, 💯going to get them to see this movie. Bring on the Jurassic finale. :D

I still remember seeing it in October of 1993. It was a warm spring night in Sydney Australia. The cinemas were completely decked out like a super hi tech zoo and the original film played around the clock to keep up with demand. It was one of the fist movies playing with DTS. We had just been awarded the right to host the Sydney 2000 olympics and no one could believe how realistic the dinosaurs were. People were talking about chaos theory, the technology of the computer systems (how cool was it that nedry could talk to the dock master while watching him on the monitor? - yes even with slider visible in the shot.

These movie sequels make me think about things. Like, I was a young teen when the first Jurassic Park came out. I have made it to the theatre to see every one since.

As funny as this sounds, I wasn’t sure I would make it to see this one (or a number of other movies like the new Batman movie), due to a number of health problems that have nearly taken me out over the years. 

Whenever a big movie series comes out over a number of years, I think, will I be alive to see the next one in the series or is this my last one? Fingers crossed I am around to catch this one, as we get closer to release day…. Weird thing to think about I know. But not all of us make it from one sequel in a series to the next. It for some reason makes me remember my mortality. 🤷‍♂️

This looks horrible actiony shlock— spare me the video game set pieces and immersion breaking cgi. 

Go watch a trailer for the original and compare it for yourself. 

This treats one of the most mysterious and beautiful realities of this world, deep time— and makes it a plot device to justify chase scenes, paint by numbers dialogue, wooden acting. The dinosaurs are props and making one have a baby doesn't imbue it with any more life. 

This, along with every other iteration since the first is just a cynical nostalgia baiting cash grab that refuses to acknowledge the reality that less is more. 

If the next movie involves strapping nuclear weapons to dinosaurs for artificial suspense I would not be surprised, that's how garbage this series has gotten. Universal you're basically the embodiment of the blood-sucking lawyer from the first. Irony abounds.

Edit after seeing the movie : What a let down ! As always the dinosaurs were amazing and I enjoyed seeing Ellie and Grant again after all these years . While there was a scene I was happy to see between Ellie and Grant they spent zero time in the movie actually rekindling anything so it sadly felt forced . The overall story of the movie was convoluted and rushed . They didn’t really explain things well enough either  and the resolution of the big problem fell flat . While never a huge Ian Malcolm fan I felt his performance in this movie was severely lack luster. The Gig really didn’t serve much of a purpose . The potential of this big bad ass dinosaur was wasted and it seemed to be in the movie just to be in the movie . Worst of all the “big” dino battle at the end we all knew was coming was over so fast it was like …really that’s it? I have loved the Jurassic Park/World movies since I was 8 years old and the first movie came out but this one left me very disappointed ☹️

Now, at 1:43 Ellie Sattler asks “What is that.” and Alan Grant replies with “The biggest Carnivore the World has ever seen”.

A lot of people are Confused with this because the Spinosaurus in JP3 was a lot bigger then Rexy and this new Giga, not to Mention that Alant Grant met the Spinosaurus in JP3 too.

Now I have a Theory.
What if the People making this Trailer are making you think they’re Talking about the Giganotosaurus but in fact they’re Talking about the Spino, it makes sense and it also makes sense for Ellie Sattler to say “What is that.” because she never saw the Spinosaurus.

To back this Theory up there was a very Short clip of the Pilot in the Plane looking at a very large Dinosaur with a Spinosaurus like Head, you couldn’t actually see the Dinosaur very well however you could see the Shape if it’s Head in the Reflection of the Plane.

Another one is in the Previous Trailer where Ian Malcom commented “Bigger, why do they always have to go Bigger.” when the Giganotosaurus appeared, but what if it’s like the same Scenario before and Ian Malcom has never seen the Spinosaurus as Well.
There’s a YouTuber who made a Video about this so please Check it out.

Now this is how I think it will go Down.
The Giga and Rexy would meet and fight each other until Rexy gets Defeated and lays on the Ground barley Alive, when this happens the Giga would walk off somewhere else until suddenly you start to see a Spine rising out of the Water. (if there even is Water)
When the Spino gets out of the Water it would slowly walk Towards Rexy with slight Hesitation and when the Spino keeps walking it would cut to that Short Clip where the Pilot was looking at it.
Then when the Spino gets close to Rexy, the Spino would look down on her and put its Foot on her Belly if you want that to Happen.
When it looks like the Spino is about to kill Rexy, the Spino stops and Hesitates to do so until the Giganotosaurus appears out of Nowhere attacking and Surprising the Spinosaurus.
When this Happens the Spino would Tumble back a bit and start to sniff and look at its Damaged Sail or Body, then the Spinosaurus would Lean Back show its True Hight and there it would cut to the Main Cast and that’s where Ellie would ask the Question and Dr Grant Answering.
Then it would cut back to the Spino letting out its Iconic JP3 Roar while Lunging Forward at the Giga, then the Spinosaurus would Tear into it until the Giga gets the upper hand and Corners it into the Water thinking it has the Advantage until the Spinosaurus Grabs the Neck of the Giga and throws itself into the Water with the Giga.
Then there would be this Underwater Fight with the Spinosaurus Winning when finally the Giga slowly lumbers out to Shore and falls on the Ground, until the Spino walks on the Shore as well looking the Giga straight in the Eye.
Now there are 2 Ways this could End, 1 the Spinosaurus grabs the Giga and Snaps it’s Neck or 2 Rexy Heals and Kills the Giga her Self.



But Hey, that just a Theory, a Jurassic Park Theory!
Thanks for Reading.

Three things about dinosaurs and birds. 1. They had lighter stronger bones which enabled the huge dinos and makes bird flight easier. 2. They had more efficient lungs which again helped the larger dinos and helps birds fly. 3. They had tighter more efficient neurones which means that despite dino fossil skulls having small brain capacity they may still have been smart like some birds are today. These 3 things suggest to me at least that dinos may have evolved from a bird like flying creature.

May be an unpopular opinion but what made the original Jurassic park work was not its big action sequences. What made the original work was the fact that humans had their survival challenged while they were out of their natural element.

What I mean is that, the original movie was believable and you genuinely feared for the characters lives as they had to survive against dinosaurs in the Dino’s environment where the Dino’s thrived. This movie seems laughable when it comes to that aspect. Your bringing dinosaurs to the mainland in countries. This isn’t believable and it looks silly. In reality, Dino’s would not be roaming around populated cities like they are. The Dino’s would be taken out quite easily since humanity is smarter and more technologically advanced. We humans would have a field day with these dinosaurs. They do not even stand a chance. The dinosaurs would be taken out or captured with ease which eliminates the horror aspect of this movie completely. It just doesn’t seem to work in my opinion. The original understood this is why the setting took place where humans did not have these advantages and where part of the plot was that the islands security system failed. In the original Allen simply could not take a machine gun and mow down the Dino’s. Here a simple call to the military would mean the end of the Dino’s in seconds. 

Not to mention the originals were clever in how they approached things. The originally movie had a purpose and had something to say about the world. This movie seems to only care about giving you the next best action scene. It’s simply eye candy and nothing else. And even the action scenes are not believable. There’s no way a flying dinosaur can take down a modern plane without getting ripped to shreds. They can’t even keep up with the planes speed. Your telling me a taradactle can fly upwards of 500 miles per hour and sink it claws into metal causing the engines to fail? What are they Superman taradactles? 

Even the original says it. The film makers here were so preoccupied with whether they could make this movie, they didn’t stop to think wether they should. 

And don’t get me started with them bringing the original cast to nostalgia bait us. Simply having these characters return won’t all of a sudden make the movie work. And the actors probably won’t put 100 percent likely being in the movie solely for the money. 

And lastly they use the pandemic as a excuse to reuse stock footage that’s edited without creating their own original scenes. This movie seems like a lazy cash grab. They are trying to present the Dino’s as a threat to humanity. This movie is best being seen by turning your brian off, saying well that was a fun 2 hours after watching then forgetting about the movie a week later. There’s a reason why the original is talked about to this day and there is a reason why most modern remakes seem to be forgotten rather quickly. 

Would it be the worst movie experience? Not necessarily. But would it have the cultural impact of the original? Nope. 

I will be treating this movie as such and waiting till it comes out to stream for free.

Why We Do Need So Many People in the World

At the start of the 20th century, the world population was roughly two billion people. Today, it is roughly eight billion. Of those eight billion, most are poor, and over a billion people are hungry to the point of starvation. Providing for so many people at a decent level seems too formidable a task for Earth. Moreover, each day, more bots, robots, and automatic machines are replacing human jobs. Humanity is producing enough to provide for everyone, so the question is why we need so many people. Would Earth, and humanity, not be better off if we were only four billion, for example?

The truth is that we wouldn’t be better off; we would be much worse off. There is a good reason for the exponential growth in humanity’s population over the past century or so.

When we look at people, we see faces and bodies, human beings, and nothing more. But the truth is much more complicated and intricate than that. Try to imagine any animal without relating it to its environment, and you will realize that it is a lost cause. The shape, color, behavior, size, longevity, and every other aspect of its existence derives from the environment where it exists. Animals are not isolated from their environment; they rely on it, follow its laws, and at the same time make up the environment that sustains them and of which they are a part.

Humans, too, make up a system. We think of ourselves as isolated individuals, but we are actually parts of a global human society. We influence it, it influences us, and almost everything that is true for the animal kingdom is true for us.

There are only two differences between animals and people, though they are fundamental differences. The first is that people are inherently mean and selfish, while animals are not. The second is that our desires grow and intensify, while those of animals remain largely the same. While we want more money and power the more we have of them, animals settle for what they have once they have had their fill.

Worse yet, from generation to generation, we are becoming more greedy, domineering, and narcissistic. Conversely, animals’ “aspirations” do not change from generation to generation: Give them enough grass, or enough game, and they are happy campers.

The reason why our desires grow while those of animals remain the same is that an increased desire makes us accelerate our development. Eventually, humans are not intended to perceive only the physical existence, but to penetrate through it and perceive the connectedness and interdependence among all of us not only intellectually, as I am explaining it here, but in the senses, just as we perceive the physical world, if not more so.

Our constant desire to search, explore, discover, and learn stems from our aspiration to know the deepest levels of reality. That knowledge is the sole prerogative of humans since only people develop such profound desires.

As our desires grow, we must learn to direct them toward discovering the purpose and structure of life. New desires first appear on the crassest level, and we must cultivate them, raise them to the level where they enhance our perception of reality.

Because these desires are so intense, we need more people to “share the load.” As just said, although we feel isolated, we are in fact one system. Everything we think is ours is actually part of the system of humanity and all of humanity shares it. Therefore, our desires are also not personal, even though they feel as such.

Every time we elevate one desire from the corporeal level to the higher levels of perception, we influence all of humanity. And every time a person dies, the burden of elevating our common desire grows heavier on all of us.

This is why the life of every person is precious. It determines the pace of advancement of all of humanity. If we realized how deeply we are connected, and what harm we cause by mistreating other people, if we felt it as it really is—that we are torturing ourselves—we would not dare mistreat each other or leave one person uncared for.

This entire trailer is basically just re-shots of the same scenes throughout the entire franchise...
0:33 - Jurassic Park 1 - When Ellie takes her glasses off when seeing the brachiosaurus first time in JP1.
0:35 - Jurassic World - When the Mosasaurus keeps popping out of the water to bite things.
0:41 - The Lost World - When Nick & Sarah release all the dinosaurs from their cages at the Ingen camp, and the Triceratops rams/turns over one of the jeeps. 
0:46 - Jurassic World - When owen saves the guy who falls into the raptor enclosure and raises both hands at the two raptors.
1:01 - The Lost World - When Eddie gets ripped in half by two T-rex
1:08 - Jurassic Park 1 - When Ellie says to John Hammond "The only thing that matters now is the people we love" (whilst they are sat at table eating icecream)
1:16 - Jurassic Park 1 - When Alan Grant says to Dr Wu "You bred raptors?"
1:24 - Jurassic Park 1 - When Ian also uses a flare to distract T-Rex who had turned over the jeep with the kids in.
1:27 - The Lost World - When Sarah Harding is crawling through the undergrowth to get a photograph of a baby stegasaurus and then gets attacked by adult stegasaurus
1:28 - The Lost World - When Ian Malcom and Nick Van Owen shout "Hang on to something" when the TRex is pushing the RV over the cliff
1:41 - Jurassic Park 1 - When Alan Grant helps Lex out of the overturned 4x4 and she suddenly screams. TRex comes and stomps it's foot into the mud.
1:46 - Jurassic Park 3 - When the walking platforms start to collapse inside the "bird cage" sequence.
1:54 - Jurassic Park 1 - When the T-Rex is chasing Ian Malcolm and T-Rex head bursts through the straw toilet building, with the lawyer sat on the toilet.

I cannot believe how bad and dumb this is plus the CGI still looks cartoony.. I loved Jurassic Park... but the world being taken over by animals the size of dinosaurs is the dumbest thing. It isn't believable. Maybe if we were talking about smaller ones that have offspring in large numbers and develop quickly like how we see snakes invading Florida. But in that example they are destroying the balance of the native ecosystem not attacking and killing humans right and left. If they were directly harming humans in cities they wouldn't last. You don't see escaped lions, tigers or elephants becoming invasive or not being captured almost immediately. They only large size animal example I can think of are the population of escaped hippos in South America. But yea this movie represents all that is wrong with Hollywood. Dried up creativity, nostalgia pandering and getting more and more extreme with every new movie. I hope this movie finally kills this franchise.
